paper_title: Multi-objective Neural Architecture Search via Non-stationary Policy Gradient

problem_statement.goal: To efficiently and accurately approximate the full Pareto front, which represents the trade-off between multiple objectives (e.g., inference time, energy consumption, accuracy, number of parameters).
challenges: The number of possible architectures is very large.

existing_approaches.name: Scalarization
existing_approaches.description: Combines multiple objectives into a single objective (e.g., using a weighted sum or product).
existing_approaches.limitation: Requires multiple runs with different scalarizations to obtain multiple Pareto-optimal solutions, which is computationally-intensive.

existing_approaches.name: Evolution (Genetic Algorithms)
existing_approaches.description: Iteratively updates a population of architectures via crossovers, mutations, and elitist selection.

examples.name: LEMONADE
examples.limitation: Approximates the Pareto front well but at a relatively high search cost.

examples.name: NSGA-Net
examples.limitation: Achieves lower cost by using low-fidelity estimates (e.g., training for only 25 epochs), which introduces high bias and can negatively impact the accuracy of the Pareto front approximation.

proposed_solution.name: Non-stationary Policy Gradient (NPG)
proposed_solution.core_idea: To continuously adapt a single policy to efficiently generate Pareto-optimal architectures, inspired by adapting policies in scalarization and evolution.
proposed_solution.framework: Built upon a Reinforcement Learning (RL) framework, leveraging a non-stationary reward function to adapt the policy continuously.
proposed_solution.advantage: Allows for the efficient and accurate approximation of the entire Pareto front in a single run.

proposed_framework.name: NPG-NAS

components.name: ADF (Annealing Desirability Function)
components.mechanism: Utilizes a target-based desirability function. It slowly anneals target values for all but one objective, gradually uncovering the entire Pareto front.
components.behavior: Traverses the entire Pareto front from left to right.

components.name: ADC (Annealing Dominance-based Reward)
components.mechanism: An architecture receives a reward based on whether it dominates or is dominated by other architectures in the current Pareto front.
components.behavior: Expands the Pareto front in bands, similar to evolutionary methods.

proposed_framework.distinction_from_evolution: Unlike evolution which uses genetic operations (crossovers, mutations) on existing individuals, NPG-NAS expands the Pareto front by sampling new architectures that are likely to dominate the current front.

title: Multi-objective Neural Architecture Search via Non-stationary Policy Gradient
